definition.geometric_coherence: c_g(N) = 1 / (1 + stress(N)), Kruskal-Shepard MDS coherence in target dimension min(3, N-1).
definition.einstein_metric_stress: sigma(N) = 1 - c_g(N) = stress(N) / (1 + stress(N)). Vanishing sigma is the obstruction-free condition for extracting a Riemannian metric on a smooth 3-manifold from the lattice distance matrix; the Einstein equations are exact only when sigma -> 0.
definition.relation_to_tensor_closure_gap: sigma(N) tracks the macroscopic tensor-closure deficit reported by the canonical multi-N pipeline (companion multi-anchor extension run), with regime-by-regime agreement to ~0.02 in absolute units. sigma(N) is the more direct definition; the canonical tensor-closure gap is a closely-related composite of the same MDS-stress geometry.
definition.relation_to_einstein_identity_gap: sigma(N) is NOT the Einstein-identity gap ||G_munu - 8 pi G T_munu|| / ||G_munu||; the latter is a separate, narrower quantity that is bundled at the two canonical regimes only, and that DOES converge under Richardson extrapolation. sigma(N) is the broader discrete-Riemannian-embedding axis that characterizes whether a Riemannian metric exists at all on the lattice; it does not vanish at the available lattice sizes.

verdict: sigma(N) = 1 - geometric_coherence holds to floating-point precision on all 7 rows; the regime profile is non-monotonic with sigma in [0.26, 0.34]; the two-point Richardson under N^(-alpha) ansatze gives sigma_inf ~ 0.30 at all three canonical exponents, i.e. no clean convergence to zero on the broad Riemannian-embedding axis (this is consistent with the canonical tensor-closure gap series).
